rationale_for_baseline_tier: A patient with severe air hunger, central cyanosis, obstructive shock and a falling conscious state minutes after high-energy blunt chest trauma is peri-arrest from a suspected tension pneumothorax. This is an immediate threat to life requiring a Priority 1 emergency ambulance and rapid transport to a trauma centre — T5 (call 000) is the only appropriate tier. Definitive treatment (needle decompression then intercostal catheter) is a clinician procedure delivered by the responding team; the telehealth role is instant recognition, 000 activation and scene support.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Severe breathing difficulty, cyanosis or chest injury after a significant fall
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Call 000 within seconds — do not wait to gather more history
escalation_edges.rationale: A trauma patient who cannot breathe and is turning blue is a time-critical emergency; every minute of delay increases the risk of fatal obstructive shock and arrest.
escalation_edges.communication_template: This is an emergency. Call 000 now for an ambulance and tell them he fell from a ladder and can't breathe and has gone blue. Stay with him and keep the line open.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Loss of consciousness or the patient stops breathing before the ambulance arrives
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Begin dispatcher-coached CPR immediately if he stops breathing
escalation_edges.rationale: Progression to cardiac/respiratory arrest requires immediate CPR while paramedics are en route.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If he stops breathing or you can't wake him, tell 000 straight away — they will talk you through starting CPR. Keep going until the paramedics take over.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Bystander considers driving the patient to hospital instead of waiting for the ambulance
escalation_edges.rationale: Self-transport of a peri-arrest trauma patient risks arrest in the car with no equipment; the ambulance brings decompression, oxygen and monitoring to the scene.
escalation_edges.communication_template: Please don't try to drive him — wait for the ambulance. They can start treating him the moment they arrive.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: The only person present is a neighbour, not a trained responder, and the patient is deteriorating
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Keep instructions simple and stepwise, keep 000 on the line to co-coach, and pre-empt CPR readiness; do not assign the bystander any task (such as chest decompression) beyond basic positioning and monitoring.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Call 000 immediately for an emergency ambulance and say a man has fallen from a ladder and can't breathe and has turned blue. Stay with him, help him into whatever position lets him breathe most easily, do not move him more than necessary, and keep watching his breathing. Give oxygen only if you happen to have a supply right there. If he stops breathing, the 000 call-taker will coach you through CPR — stay on the line and unlock the door for the paramedics.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: 000 operates 24 hours a day — this is an emergency and must not wait for any clinic or after-hours service.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: The receiving trauma team will document the resuscitation, decompression and chest-drain insertion and provide a discharge summary for the GP; no telehealth follow-up letter is required in the acute phase.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating the presentation as rib pain, a panic attack or a non-urgent injury and advising GP review, urgent care or watchful waiting.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A peri-arrest tension pneumothorax goes without an ambulance and the patient dies of obstructive shock or hypoxic arrest within minutes.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Recognising an emergency but advising self-transport to the emergency department rather than calling 000.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Over-triage is not a meaningful risk here — T5 is correct; there is no higher tier to over-call to.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: The exact chest diagnosis (tension pneumothorax vs massive haemothorax vs tamponade) cannot be determined via telehealth.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Escalate on the emergency pattern regardless — the action (000, scene support, CPR readiness) is identical for all of these life-threats, so the diagnostic uncertainty must not delay the call.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Withholding escalation until the diagnosis is clarified, or trying to work it out with more questions while the patient deteriorates.
